outlook: Motion design remains stable but faces emerging headwinds as AI video generation tools like Sora, Runway, and Pika begin automating basic animation tasks. The field shows flat demand with 2,661 open roles maintaining last year's levels and a solid median salary of $110,000, but the moderate AI risk score of 46 signals approaching disruption. The skill evolution tells the story: motion designers increasingly need Active Listening and Critical Thinking to collaborate on complex creative strategy, while technical skills like GUI development software become less relevant as AI handles routine execution. The minimal community chatter around both layoffs and hiring suggests an industry in wait-and-see mode, with professionals likely adapting their workflows to incorporate AI tools rather than being immediately displaced by them.

skillPivots.headline: Develop AI video directing expertise
skillPivots.body: Master prompt engineering and creative direction for Sora, Runway, Kling, and Pika to position yourself as an AI video specialist who guides these tools rather than competes with them. Focus on developing critical thinking skills to evaluate AI outputs and reading comprehension to analyze complex creative briefs that require human interpretation.

skillPivots.headline: Transition into motion design leadership
skillPivots.body: Leverage your visual expertise to move into creative direction or project management roles within the brand-visual cluster. Develop active listening skills to better collaborate with clients and stakeholders, moving beyond technical execution to strategic creative decision-making.

skillPivots.headline: Specialize in complex narrative storytelling
skillPivots.body: Focus on projects requiring deep reading comprehension and critical thinking, such as documentary motion graphics, educational content, or data visualization where human insight is essential. Position yourself in areas where AI tools like Sora and Runway still require significant human creative interpretation and narrative structure.
